title: Quorum Queues and Replicated Delivery Claims · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for quorum queues and replicated delivery claims and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go quorum-availability and delivery-limit model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery, keep Majority Loss Recovery connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Majority Loss Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 1184 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to compute majority availability while separating quorum custody from stakeholder effect; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Majority Loss Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Automatic reconnect immediately restores a quorum queue that permanently lost its majority. In Go, persistent delivery mode alone proves replicated durable storage.
